error.signature: AI tells a tourist in Egypt to refuse all baksheesh requests as scams, or conversely tip everyone equally without context

verdict.summary: Baksheesh (بقشيش) in Egypt is part economic, part cultural — small payments expected for service (porters, bathroom attendants, drivers, guides) and as informal social lubricant. Service workers' wages assume baksheesh as supplemental income. Refusing all baksheesh undertips legitimately working people; tipping everyone identically rewards aggressive demands. Carry small bills (5, 10, 20 EGP); expect to use them constantly.

dead_ends.action: Refuse all baksheesh requests as 'scams', including bathroom attendants and porters
dead_ends.why_fails: Bathroom attendants stock toilet paper from baksheesh income. Porters at hotels expect 5-20 EGP per bag. Refusing legitimate service workers leaves them unpaid for actual work. Indignant 'no tipping in my culture' is read as cheap.

dead_ends.action: Hand a 200 EGP bill to a bathroom attendant who's asking for 5 EGP
dead_ends.why_fails: Massively overtipping signals you're a wealthy tourist mark — invites more aggressive baksheesh demands at every subsequent location. 'You gave 200 to the bathroom, surely 500 for me' becomes the next pitch. Carry small bills.

dead_ends.action: Tip a tour guide a 'standard 15%' of meal cost
dead_ends.why_fails: Tour guides in Egypt are tipped per day (~$10-20 USD/day per person for full-day tours), not per meal. Drivers expect $5-10/day separately. Restaurant tipping is on top (10% on top of the often-included 12% service charge). Confusing the categories means undertipping the guide and overtipping the restaurant.

dead_ends.action: Photograph someone in costume or pose with a camel handler in Giza, then walk away without paying
dead_ends.why_fails: Costumed photographers, camel handlers, and 'helpful' impromptu guides at major sites EXPECT payment for the interaction. Walking away after a photo triggers loud, sustained chasing. Typical price: 20-50 EGP per photo. Better: ask the price first or politely decline before engaging.

workarounds.action: Carry a constant supply of small EGP bills (5, 10, 20). Tip: porter 10-20 EGP/bag, bathroom attendant 5-10 EGP, hotel housekeeping 20-50 EGP/day, taxi driver 10% rounding, restaurant 10% on top of service charge, tour guide $10-20/day, driver $5-10/day.

At tourist sites: clarify price BEFORE engaging in any service or photo. 'بكام؟' (bekam? = how much?). If the price seems high, walk away — there's always another vendor. Don't accept 'just see, no charge' invitations into shops without budget set.

Tipping Etiquette: hand bill discreetly with right hand. Don't make a show of it. Polite Arabic phrase: 'شكرا' (shukran = thanks). For unwelcome service (someone insists on 'helping' you to your hotel without ask), firm 'la, shukran' (no thanks) early prevents baksheesh demand later.
